BaseMessage<MessageStructure<MessageTool.description:
Base class for all types of messages in a conversation. It includes
properties like `content`, `name`, and `additional_kwargs`. It also
includes methods like `toDict()` and `_getType()`.

lc_namespace.description:
A path to the module that contains the class, eg. ["langchain", "llms"]
Usually should be the same as the entrypoint the class is exported from.

lc_aliases.description:
A map of aliases for constructor args.
Keys are the attribute names, e.g. "foo".
Values are the alias that will replace the key in serialization.
This is used to eg. make argument names match Python.

id.description: Unique identifier for this message

name.description:
An optional name for the message participant.

This property is primarily used to:

1. **Identify agent roles in multi-agent systems**: When multiple agents
   collaborate, setting `name` helps distinguish which agent produced a
   message, preventing confusion about who said what.

2. **Pass participant names to model providers**: Some providers (notably
   OpenAI, e.g. see {@link https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create OpenAI Chat Completions API})
   use this field to differentiate between participants with the
   same role. For example, when using OpenAI's Chat Completions API, the
   `name` is included in the message payload sent to the model.

content.description: Array of content blocks that make up the message content

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

function.description: The function that the model called.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

text.description: Get text content of the message.

lc_id.description: The final serialized identifier for the module.

lc_secrets.description:
A map of secrets, which will be omitted from serialization.
Keys are paths to the secret in constructor args, e.g. "foo.bar.baz".
Values are the secret ids, which will be used when deserializing.

lc_attributes.description:
A map of additional attributes to merge with constructor args.
Keys are the attribute names, e.g. "foo".
Values are the attribute values, which will be serialized.
These attributes need to be accepted by the constructor as arguments.

lc_serializable_keys.description:
A manual list of keys that should be serialized.
If not overridden, all fields passed into the constructor will be serialized.

MessageType.description:
Represents the possible types of messages in the system.
Includes standard message types ("ai", "human", "tool", "system")
and allows for custom string types that are non-null.

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

ContentBlock.Text.description:
Text output from a LLM.

This typically represents the main text content of a message, such as the response
from a language model or the text of a user message.

index.description: Index of block in aggregate response. Used during streaming.

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

ContentBlock.Citation.description: Annotation for citing data from a document.

title.description:
Source document title.

For example, the page title for a web page or the title of a paper.

startIndex.description: Start index of the **response text** for which the annotation applies.

endIndex.description: End index of the **response text** for which the annotation applies.

citedText.description: Excerpt of source text being cited.

arguments.description:
The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON
format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may
hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the
arguments in your code before calling your function.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the file.

fileId.description: ID of the data file, e.g. from a provider's file api

mimeType.description: MIME type of the file. Required for base64 encoding.

id.description:
Content block identifier for multimodal content, e.g. image, video, audio, file or plain text. This can be either:
 - generated by the provider (e.g., an OpenAI block ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation

mimeType.description: MIME type of the file.
MIME type of the file. Required for base64 encoding.

 - generated by LangChain upon creation

ContentBlock.NonStandard<Record<string,a.description:
Provider-specific content block.

This is used to represent content blocks that are not part of the standard LangChain content model.
If a provider's non-standard output includes reasoning and tool calls, it should be
the adapter's job to parse that payload and emit the corresponding standard reasoning and tool call blocks.

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

name.description: The name of the tool being called

args.description: The arguments to the tool call

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

Tools.ToolCallChunk<string>.description: Content block to represent partial data of a tool call

name.description: The name of the tool being called

args.description: The arguments to the tool call

index.description: The index of the tool call chunk

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

args.description: The arguments to the tool call

error.description: An error message associated with the tool call

 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

toolCallId.description: The unique identifier of the tool call that this result corresponds to

status.description: The status of the server tool call

output.description: The output of the server tool call

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

2. **Pass participant names to model providers**: Some providers (notably
   OpenAI, e.g. see {@link https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create OpenAI Chat Completions API})
   use this field to differentiate between participants with the
   same role. For example, when using OpenAI's Chat Completions API, the
   `name` is included in the message payload sent to the model.

content.description: Array of content blocks that make up the message content

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

function.description: The function that the model called.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

text.description: Get text content of the message.

lc_id.description: The final serialized identifier for the module.

lc_secrets.description:
A map of secrets, which will be omitted from serialization.
Keys are paths to the secret in constructor args, e.g. "foo.bar.baz".
Values are the secret ids, which will be used when deserializing.

lc_attributes.description:
A map of additional attributes to merge with constructor args.
Keys are the attribute names, e.g. "foo".
Values are the attribute values, which will be serialized.
These attributes need to be accepted by the constructor as arguments.

lc_serializable_keys.description:
A manual list of keys that should be serialized.
If not overridden, all fields passed into the constructor will be serialized.

MessageType.description:
Represents the possible types of messages in the system.
Includes standard message types ("ai", "human", "tool", "system")
and allows for custom string types that are non-null.

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

ContentBlock.Text.description:
Text output from a LLM.

This typically represents the main text content of a message, such as the response
from a language model or the text of a user message.

index.description: Index of block in aggregate response. Used during streaming.

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

ContentBlock.Citation.description: Annotation for citing data from a document.

title.description:
Source document title.

For example, the page title for a web page or the title of a paper.

startIndex.description: Start index of the **response text** for which the annotation applies.

endIndex.description: End index of the **response text** for which the annotation applies.

citedText.description: Excerpt of source text being cited.

arguments.description:
The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON
format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may
hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the
arguments in your code before calling your function.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the file.

fileId.description: ID of the data file, e.g. from a provider's file api

mimeType.description: MIME type of the file. Required for base64 encoding.

reasoning.description:
Reasoning text.

Either the thought summary or the raw reasoning text itself.
This is often parsed from `<think>` tags in the model's response.

index.description: Index of block in aggregate response. Used during streaming.

Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

ContentBlock.NonStandard<Record<string,a.description:
Provider-specific content block.

This is used to represent content blocks that are not part of the standard LangChain content model.
If a provider's non-standard output includes reasoning and tool calls, it should be
the adapter's job to parse that payload and emit the corresponding standard reasoning and tool call blocks.

id.description:
Content block identifier, which can be either
 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

Tools.ToolCall<string,unknown>.description: Represents a request to call a tool.

name.description: The name of the tool being called

args.description: The arguments to the tool call

 - generated by the provider (e.g., a provider-specific ID)
 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')

args.description: The arguments to the tool call

 - generated by LangChain upon creation (a uuid prefixed with 'lc-')
